2606.29980.title: Exploration and Online Transfer with Behavioral Foundation Models
2606.29980.paper_summary: Zero-shot Transfer in Reinforcement Learning (RL) aims to train an agent that can generate optimal policies for any reward function, without additional learning at transfer time, while training only on reward-free trajectories. For their generality over tasks, such models are sometimes called ``Behavioral Foundation Models'' (BFMs). While they have shown strong performances and improvements in recent years, the current framework and algorithms still assume that, during the transfer phase, the agent is informed offline about the reward (the task to solve) through a dataset of state-reward pairs, which it uses to pick the best policy to deploy. However, in practice if the reward is a black-box (e.g. direct user feedback), it is not possible to generate such a dataset: it is necessary to observe the reward through interactions with the environment. In other words, the current framework of offline transfer is not aligned with the traditional RL setting of online learning through trial-and-error, which requires exploration in order to find rewards. This paper proposes to tackle this new online transfer in zero-shot RL, with the key insight that the BFM itself can be used to generate exploration policies. We show that it is possible to frame this online learning problem in terms of a bandit-like exploration-exploitation problem. More precisely, at each step the bandit algorithm recommends a policy, the BFM executes it in the environment, which yields a reward and a new state; we repeat the process until we converge to the optimal policy. In the popular context of linear reward approximation, we derive a formulation inspired by Upper Confidence Bound and show that exploration can be achieved through the minimization of the eigenvalues of an uncertainty matrix. We evaluate qualitatively and quantitatively our framework on a simple environment to validate the concept of our method.
2606.29980.paper_summary_zh: 零-shot 轉移在強化學習 (RL) 中旨在訓練一個代理，該代理可以為任何獎勵函數生成最佳策略，而無需在轉移時進行額外學習，並且僅在無獎勵的軌跡上進行訓練。由於這些模型在任務上的通用性，它們有時被稱為「行為基礎模型」(BFMs)。雖然它們在近年來顯示出強大的表現和改進，但目前的框架和算法仍然假設在轉移階段，代理是通過一組狀態-獎勵對的數據集離線獲得獎勵（要解決的任務）的信息，並利用這些信息選擇最佳策略進行部署。然而，實際上如果獎勵是黑箱（例如直接用戶反饋），則無法生成這樣的數據集：必須通過與環境的互動來觀察獎勵。換句話說，目前的離線轉移框架與通過試錯進行在線學習的傳統 RL 設置並不一致，後者需要探索以找到獎勵。本文提出解決零-shot RL 中的新在線轉移，關鍵見解是 BFM 本身可以用來生成探索策略。我們表明，可以將這個在線學習問題框架化為類似賭徒的探索-利用問題。更精確地說，在每一步中，賭徒算法推薦一個策略，BFM 在環境中執行該策略，這會產生一個獎勵和一個新狀態；我們重複這個過程，直到收斂到最佳策略。在流行的線性獎勵近似的背景下，我們推導出一個受上置信界啟發的公式，並顯示可以通過最小化不確定性矩陣的特徵值來實現探索。我們在一個簡單的環境中對我們的框架進行了定性和定量評估，以驗證我們方法的概念。
2606.29980.author: Louis Bagot et.al.
2606.29980.authors: Louis Bagot, Mathieu Lefort, Laëtitia Matignon
